title: What Nobody Warns You About Payment Infrastructure for South Asian Creators
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text:
If you're a creator in South Asia, you're likely no stranger to the frustration of trying to sell digital products through platforms like PayPal, Stripe, or Gumroad – only to be blocked due to geographic restrictions or KYC requirements. This is not just an annoyance; it can be a business-killing problem. In this post, we'll expose the hidden costs of relying on these platforms and show you how to build a store that can't be taken down by restrictive payment systems."

  "sections": [
    { "heading": "The Mistake Almost Every Creator Makes First
The Mistake Almost Every Creator Makes First

Many creators make the mistake of thinking that they need to jump through hoops to get approved by these platforms, or that they're somehow exempt from KYC requirements. But the truth is, these platforms have strict rules in place, and getting blocked is often just a matter of time. In India, for example, many creators have reported being blocked by PayPal without warning or explanation, resulting in lost sales and revenue.
Why This Keeps Happening to Good Stores

The reason why creators keep getting blocked is that these platforms are designed to prioritize their own interests over those of their users. They're not interested in supporting creators from restricted regions – they're just looking to maximize their own profits. As a result, creators are left to fend for themselves, struggling to find new ways to get paid for their work.
The Real Cost of Getting This Wrong

The real cost of getting blocked by these platforms is not just financial – it's also a matter of reputation and credibility. When creators can't get paid for their work, it undermines their entire business model and makes it harder to build trust with their audience. In Pakistan, for example, many creators have reported losing thousands of dollars in revenue due to PayPal blocking their accounts.
Creators in South Asia are not alone in their struggles with payment infrastructure – many creators around the world face similar challenges.
How to Protect Yourself Before It Happens

So how can you protect yourself from getting blocked by these platforms? The answer is simple: build a store that doesn't rely on them. With tools like Web3 Wallet Connect, you can integrate multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds into your store, allowing buyers to pay in a wide range of cryptocurrencies. This way, you're not beholden to any one platform or payment processor.
Building the Store That Cannot Be Taken Down

Building a store that can't be taken down requires a few key steps. First, choose a payment processor that doesn't require KYC or have geographic restrictions – such as NOWPayments. Next, integrate a frontend wallet like Web3 Wallet Connect to allow buyers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency. Finally, make sure to have a solid understanding of your target audience and their payment preferences.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

What about creators in Africa who want to sell digital products internationally? The answer is simple: use a payment processor like NOWPayments that doesn't have geographic restrictions, and integrate a frontend wallet like Web3 Wallet Connect to allow buyers to pay in their preferred cryptocurrency."
